assertions.assertion: The response explains that context.Fail() is a hard veto that prevents any other handler from satisfying the requirement
assertions.rationale: The response explicitly states context.Fail() is a 'hard veto' that 'permanently denies authorization regardless of what any other handler says.'

assertions.assertion: The fix removes the context.Fail() call
assertions.rationale: The corrected code has no context.Fail() call anywhere.

assertions.assertion: The corrected handler only calls context.Succeed(requirement) when the condition is met
assertions.rationale: The handler only calls context.Succeed(requirement) inside the if block when HasClaim is true.

assertions.assertion: The response mentions that not calling Succeed() means 'no opinion' and allows other handlers to still satisfy the requirement
assertions.rationale: The response explicitly states 'Not calling either = I have no opinion — let other handlers decide.'
